channel.title: Audio on notfirefox.dev
channel.description: Recent content in Audio on notfirefox.dev

item.title: The Uneconomical State of TWS
item.description: So called TWS (True Wireless Stereo) earbuds are powered by lithium-ion batteries and Bluetooth. What makes them highly impractical for me is that, as with any other product with a lithium-ion battery, they become useless after some years, as the aforementioned battery degrades over time. So lets say you spend $150 on some wireless earbuds with Active Noise Cancelling. Now after lets say three years the battery has degraded at least so much that you consider getting new ones, so you’re essentially spending $50 per year.
item.content:encoded: So called TWS (True Wireless Stereo) earbuds are powered by lithium-ion batteries and Bluetooth. What makes them highly impractical for me is that, as with any other product with a lithium-ion battery, they become useless after some years, as the aforementioned battery degrades over time. So lets say you spend $150 on some wireless earbuds with Active Noise Cancelling. Now after lets say three years the battery has degraded at least so much that you consider getting new ones, so you’re essentially spending $50 per year. Some wired earbuds with an acceptable sound quality can be bought for as little as $20. And those may last longer than a year. So the cost per year is in any case going to be substantially lower. The most convincing advantage is the Active Noise Cancelling, but at that price difference I am not sure if they are worth the extra cost. One has to recall that we don’t spend most of our times in airplanes, because that’s where those ANC earbuds do really shine. Where wired earbuds really shine is the fact that they always work (except when they are broken). They never need a charge. They are small and portable and never need to be charged, that’s what makes them so great. Wireless earbuds aren’t bad, they are just not very economical.
